Seishan had not been rattled by the deep wound on her back, but at that moment, she felt her blood run cold.
She hoped that Revel was safe.
Taking a careful step back, Seishan looked at her sisters with unease.
"Snap out of it, you fools. I am not going to say it twice."

When they were little kids, this stern tone usually got them to behave. Seishan was not older than the rest of her sisters, but she had played the mother role in their close-knit group of orphan girls. She had been their leader, all those years ago… and they looked up to her still.
But the bond they shared did not seem to matter anymore.
Beastmaster sighed.
"I am sorry, Shan. I really am. But… you will understand soon…"
They lunged at her.
The beautiful moons shone upon the desolate plain, and the nearby canyons roared as mystical rivers rushed through them.
On that night, Seishan — the Lost Princess of Song — spilled the blood of her sisters.

Their battle was short and furious, its dreadful violence leaving deep scars on the surface of the rocky plain. Seishan's sisters were skilled and powerful — they were the best of the best, in fact, more than worthy of being heirs of the Great Clan Song.
But she was stronger, more skilled, and far more ruthless.
More than that, she was the one who had inherited the core of Beast God's Lineage from their mother… [The Blood].
And by inheriting it, she had also inherited power over all others who had Beast God's blood in their veins.
So, in the end, her sisters lay on the ground, bleeding, but Seishan was still standing.
Barely standing, but not on her knees.
She swayed, raising a trembling hand to wipe the blood off her face.
'Ah…'
Her mind was empty.
'I am going to lose, aren't I?'
Her sisters were broken and battered, but they were still alive. That was because she couldn't bring herself to kill them.
And because she couldn't kill them, they were going to bring her down eventually.
"Help…"
Beastmaster's voice was weak.
Seishan shuddered.
"Bin!"
She staggered toward Beastmaster and fell to her knees near her, full of guilt and concern.
Then, she slapped her sister across the face.
"Don't you try that with me. Get out of my head!"
The guilt, the concern — all of it was nothing but a mental attack disguised as a flood of genuine emotions. Seishan knew that the wounds she had dealt to the beautiful enchantress were not fatal.
Beastmaster grinned weakly.
"Or what? What will you do?"
Seishan raised a hand, her nails extending to turn into sharp claws.
But she never brought it down.
Beastmaster, meanwhile, reached forward and thrust her fingers into a ghastly wound on Seishan's side, ripping it open.
"What can you do, Shan? Give it up… or kill us. One or the other — there is nothing else you can do."
She was right.
Seishan could not even escape, because a few of her sisters were much faster than she was.
She stared down at Beastmaster, her claws still hovering above her sister's throat.
All Seishan needed to do was slash across Beastmaster's delicate neck once. If she wanted to escape, she needed to deal with her sisters. Otherwise, a fate worse than death awaited her.
They had all been taken by Asterion already, anyway.
But her hand refused to move.
In the end, Seishan looked at the three beautiful moons and laughed.
Then, she sobbed.
"I'm just like our mother, in the end…"
Lowering her hand, Seishan bent down and pressed her forehead against the ground.
"I can't, I can't… I can't kill them…"
She surrendered.

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2819.txt
content: In another memory, Seishan was sitting on her mother's throne.

She wore a regal red gown, leaning languidly against the throne's backrest. The vast hall was illuminated by the ghostly light of dancing flames, and in that light, her pale grey skin made her look like a corpse.
Her scarlet lips were moist, as if covered in fresh blood.

Now, finally, she had reclaimed her childhood home… just like her mother reclaimed it from the traitors who had usurped it once, all those years ago. The Jade Palace once again belonged to a descendant of Ravenheart, as it was meant to.
Two of her Blood Sisters were standing silently behind the throne. Below the throne, Beastmaster stood leisurely, making a report.

"...The Chained Isles are firmly in his hands, but his advance south is slowing down. The Black Mountains serve as a natural barrier, and every mountain pass there is now a battlefield. Here in the West, however, the situation is more difficult."
She sighed.

"He doesn't seem to be in a hurry to besiege Ravenheart, but his raids on the cities in the basin of the River of Tears are growing bolder with each passing day. He comes and goes out of reflections, so we cannot give chase. His forces are spread thin, but no matter what losses he suffers in the Black Mountains, Godgrave provides him with plenty of new vessels to continue the invasion. In fact, the number of his vessels is growing."
Sieshan remained silent for a while, frowning.
"I presume he is still building up his forces while avoiding a direct clash against our lord."
Beastmaster nodded.
"Indeed. That is most likely the reason why he has not besieged Ravenheart yet."

Ravenheart was not an easy target even for Mordret. There were too many Saints, Masters, and Awakened here — more importantly than that, committing to this battle would put the besieging vessel army at risk of being culled by the Dreamspawn.
The Hunger Domain encompassed most of humanity by now, while the Mirror Domain was still relatively small and weak.
However, it was growing stronger every day.
Seishan's frown deepened.
"There needs to be a decisive battle."
Beastmaster grimaced.
"It's not that I disagree, but how are we supposed to force such a battle on him?"
Seishan hesitated. She was not quite sure herself.
But just then, when she felt mired in doubt, it was as if a subtle voice whispered into her ear. New thoughts were instilled in her mind.
Seishan smiled.
"We cannot force him to commit to a battle, true. However, we can invite him to give battle with a proper lure."
Beastmaster raised an eyebrow.
"What can that slippery man find so irresistible as to abandon caution?"
Seishan did not answer for a while, and a golden gleam seemed to shine in her eyes for a short moment.

"We have his sister, do we not? He already allowed somebody else to kill his father. I doubt that he will tolerate someone else killing his sister, as well. If we proceed slowly enough, he will most likely show up before we finish the job."
There was a muffled sound, but she ignored it before adding:
"And if that doesn't help, we will just have to make Ravenheart vulnerable. If he sees a real chance to destroy the city, he will act despite the danger."
Seishan paused, then smiled and asked in a gentle tone:
"What do you think, Kai? Do you like this plan?"
She looked down, where a man was kneeling beneath the throne, shackled and chained to the floor. He was pale and disheveled, looking at her with a scathing, sorrowful emotion in his sunken eyes.
There was a silk scarf tied around his neck, the white fabric painted rusty-brown and vibrant scarlet by layers of dried and fresh blood.
He stared silently at her, offering no answer.
Well, of course, he didn't answer. After all, she had torn out his tongue.

At first, Changing Star's flames had attempted to heal him, but after Seishan repeated the painful process a few times, they subsided. It seemed like Nephis had realized that healing this particular wound would only subject Kai to more torment.
Looking at him, Seishan smiled sweetly.
"It doesn't seem like you like my plan very much. But do not worry…"
She licked her scarlet lips and chuckled.
"Even if Ravenheart falls... it will be for the greater good."
Kai strained against his chains — but, of course, it was of no use.
In the end, exhausted and dizzy, he tiredly closed his eyes.
…In a different memory, Kai was standing at the gates of the Jade Palace, looking at the entrance to the great bridge in front of him with a tired, but determined expression.
Out there on the bridge, bodies littered the ground. Above them, thousands of Awakened warriors were frozen in place, utterly motionless, their hair dancing in the frigid wind.

Some seemed to have frozen midstep as they were rushing toward the Jade Palace, some were kneeling on the ground. Some were brandishing their weapons, while others were reaching for their quivers, ready to knock arrows on the strings of their bows.
They did not move.
They could not move because he had commanded them to cease moving. The power of his voice bound them like an invisible chain, and none of them was strong enough to resist it.
Well… almost.
At the very edge of the great bridge, six women were arrayed like a crescent in front of Kai, looking at him with calm expressions.
"Impressive."
Seishan offered him a strained, but graceful bow.
"But how long can you keep this up, Kai?"
He gritted his teeth silently, prompting her to laugh.
"This Citadel… it rightfully belongs to me. To us. What right do you have to occupy our mother's home? To steal our inheritance?"
Shaking her head, she strained a little and then pushed her body one step forward.
"I've come to claim what is mine, Kai. Do not stand in my way."
He frowned
"Stand down, Seishan. Even if you defeat me and claim the Jade Palace, it won't belong to you or your sisters. It will only belong to the Dreamspawn."
As the wind picked up, his eyes glistened.
"Not to mention that you can't defeat me. Not now, not here — not as long as I am the master of this Citadel."

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2820.txt
content: Kai was strong enough to contend against Seishan. He wasn't alone in this fight, either.
Summer Knight stood just behind him, studying Seishan and her sisters impassively.

Ironically enough, out of all the people in Ravenheart, it was Sir Gilead who had turned out to be the most impervious to the plague of Asterion. The man's single-minded loyalty to the king whom he had sworn to serve was so uncompromising that he refused to submit to anyone else.
He had refused to accept Changing Star as his new Sovereign, and now, he was refusing to bow down to the Dreamspawn as well.

There were a few others who were still holding out against the plague, too. Most of the city had already become enthralled, though… and Asterion wasn't satisfied simply letting the plague spread anymore.
Instead, he wanted the Saints he had successfully subjugated to conquer the Citadels still belonging to the Longing Domain.

So, his thralls had been besieging the Jade Palace under Seishan's command for the last few days. Despite their great numerical advantage, the great bridge connecting the snowy mountain to the volcano was a natural bottleneck that prevented them from descending on the Citadel like a swarm.

Still, Kai would have long lost the battle… if not for the gargantuan steel colossus that had risen from the volcano like an infernal monument to hunger and sin when Seishan first led her troops to capture the Jade Palace,

The towering body of the colossus was entirely black, covered in numerous blades and spikes that protruded from it like a graveyard of shattered swords. The flames of hell burned in his dreadful eyes, and when he opened the incinerating furnace of his maw, unbearable heat washed over the surroundings, turning snow into flowing water.
He was the Supreme Devil whom the Lord of Shadow had left behind to defend Ravenheart.
With the help of this fiend, Kai had been able to repel Seishan's attacks, ending each battle in a draw.
The Devil was still with him, guarding the gates of the Jade Palace.
Seishan regarded it with a somber expression.
Silent Stalker did, as well, since she had fought it before — on the shores of the Vanishing Lake, during the war in Godgrave.
They were at a stalemate.
Seishan remained silent for a long while and then said evenly:
"Did you say that I would not be able to defeat you as long as you rule the Jade Palace, Kai?"
She smiled suddenly.
"I tend to disagree. However, if I continue this siege, the King of Nothing will easily make use of our disagreement. So, I'll just have to make you surrender the Jade Palace of your own free will."
Kai raised an eyebrow.
"And how exactly are you planning to do that?"
Seishan's exquisite smile widened slightly.
"By the grace of Lord Asterion, of course."
Kai studied her for a few moments, and then frowned.
"Sorry, but I don't think I'll be surrendering the Jade Palace to you, Seishan."
She chuckled.
"You did not think that you would call upon Lord Asterion in a moment of need either, did you?"
Summer Knight shifted uncomfortably.
"Don't listen to her, Nightingale. She is just trying to rattle you and get into your head."
Seishan took a step back and raised her hands in a graceful gesture.
"Behold the grace of our lord."
Her voice sounded reverent.
Responding to it, there was movement on the bridge.

The great bridge was so long and vast that several districts of the city had been built on its surface and on the platforms suspended from it, serving as home to most of the Awakened who lived in Ravenheart.
Now, countless people flowed onto it, flooding it like a dark tide.
Kai tensed, wondering if Seishan was sending all her reserves into one massive, reckless assault.

However, the people on the bridge did not join the battle. Many of them did not even look like warriors — in fact, they were mundane citizens, unremarkable and all but useless in a battle between Awakened.
"What are you…"
Instead of trying to attack the Jade Palace, they slowly spread along the edges of the great bridge, forming two columns that stretched from one of its ends to the other.
And then, silently, they climbed to stand on the parapets.
Kai paled.
"What about now, Kai? Do you feel like surrendering yet?"
Seishan's pleasant, raspy voice made him shiver.
Not receiving an answer, she sighed.
"Be it your way."
Kai flinched.

Summer Knight could not see, but he witnessed it in flawless detail. Far in the distance, the two people closest to the opposite end of the bridge silently took a step forward and plummeted into the bottomless chasm below.
Their silhouettes disappeared into the ash blizzard a few moments later, as if erased from existence.
That was what it looked like to everyone but Kai, who could easily see through the ash.
Seishan looked at him with a smile.
"How about now?"
The next pair fell down.
"Not yet?"
Kai simply stood there, frozen.
"Stop."
Seishan sighed.
"I'm afraid I cannot stop."
Two more people plummeted to their deaths.
"Stop."
She looked at him with pity.
"Only you can stop this, Kai."
Two more.
And then, another two.
And then...
"Stop. Stop!"
Seishan laughed.
"They will only stop when you surrender. Are you still hesitating?"
Kai shuddered.
She let out a quiet sigh.
"Look. Your hesitation has already cost dozens of lives."
Seishan looked at him, the smile leaving her scarlet lips.
"How many more people must die because of your inaction?"
Kai simply stood there, frozen… just like the people whom he had commanded to halt were frozen in front of him, unable to move.
The steel colossus of the Lord of Shadows remained motionless, as well, awaiting Kai's command.
By his side, Summer Knight spared the Steward of the West a grim look, then gritted his teeth and lunged forward.
His sword was aimed at Seishan, but it never reached her.
Her sisters moved to intercept him, batting the sword away and raining blows on his armor.
"You… wretched…"

Gilead would not be swayed by the plague… which made him incapable of becoming a subject for the Hunger Domain, and therefore useless to Asterion. So, the Song sisters did not hold back their killing intent.
At the same time, he was not a part of the Longing Domain. So, Changing Star could not heal his wounds.
He should have been crushed under an avalanche of their attacks… but, somehow, Gilead broke through the barrier of steel created by the Song sisters and rushed toward Seishan.
His armor was broken and torn, but his body was only covered by minor lacerations. His sword was still sharp and steady.
Seishan tilted her head, looking at him.
As Summer Knight took the next step, the minor wounds on his body began to bleed.
A step more, and the trickles of blood that flowed from his wounds turned into streams.
Another step, and his bleeding turned gruesomely dire.
Summer Knight slowed down, swaying, then gritted his teeth and took the next step.
By the time he reached Seishan, he resembled a ghastly red statue. The remains of his polished armor were painted entirely red, and his face was like a glistening crimson mask.
"He said… stop…"
Despite Summer Knight's horrid state, his low voice remained clear.
His sword trembled as it rose arduously, straining to reach Seishan's neck.
But in the end, it clattered to the ground.
Seishan did not move, but Summer Knight swayed, then fell down and grew still, the crown of his head resting near her feet.
A puddle of blood spread around his body, soaking the hem of her dress.
And behind her, two more people stopped off the bridge into the ashen abyss.
Kai looked down, dazed.
"Gilead…"
There was…
There was so much blood.
...That day, Seishan of Clan Song became the master of the Jade Palace.

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2821.txt
content: In another memory, Soul Reaper Jet was looking down at the vast expanse of the Night Garden as life slowly flowed out of her broken soul, drop by drop — like it always did.
Bringing a golden fruit to her mouth, she took a bite and chewed its flesh slowly, a thoughtful expression on her face.
She spared the fruit a long look.
"Eating fruits every day..."
She had never even seen a fruit during her time in the outskirts, let alone tasted one. Life was sure full of surprises. It was full of unexpected outcomes, as well.
Taking another bite, she continued to study the lively expanse of the layered decks far below.
Things were turning strange on the Night Garden.

The ship was currently gliding through the boundless abyss of the dark sky below the Chained Isles. There was nothing but an endless emptiness above it. Beneath it, meanwhile, was a ferocious sea of fire— it was as if the Night Garden was sailing above the surface of a star.

The heat rising from the ocean of divine flame was keeping the titanic ship afloat in the empty abyss. Not in the sense that it was gliding on the rising currents of warm air, though — rather, the hull of the Night Garden was soaking in the heat and turning it into torrents of essence to sustain its innate sorcery.

Which was good news, since the ship was consuming a great amount of essence now that it was sailing on air instead of water. That was something it had become capable of after being mended and restored at the wharf of the Eternal City, among other things.

Making the Night Garden fly had actually turned out to be quite simple. It had never relied on the winds or propulsion engines to sail, after all — instead, it moved due to the divine sorcery left behind by the Demon of Repose. So, if its helmsman was skilled and powerful enough, the living ship could easily sail on air instead of water... or even on nothing at all, if need be.

It was just that the amount of essence required to lift it into the air was too taxing for a Transcendent captain. Beings of such a measly Rank were quite obviously not meant to control the living vessel, and since they could not substitute the essence the Night Garden consumed with their own, an external source was needed to keep it in the air for prolonged periods of time. Something like a raging storm... or a sea of divine flames burning in a vast abyss.

The mission to evacuate the citizens of the Sanctuary of Noctis had been both sudden and ambitious. The Night Garden traveled to the Chained Isles through the Dream Gate, steered by the Saints of Night — Naeve, Aether, Bloodwave, and Nightwalker himself. With the four of them rotating at the helm, the living ship could barely be kept afloat.

The Night Garden arrived at the Sanctuary — the city that now sprawled across half a dozen islands around the Sanctuary of Noctis — where the entire local population hastily boarded it. Saint Tyris and Saint Roan, as well as the Awakened warriors of the White Feather clan, joined them as well. Then, the living ship dove into the Sky Below just before the King of Nothing descended upon the Chained Isles from the Hollow Mountains.
And here they were, drifting above the sea of flames. Nephis had departed to wherever it was she had gone, and the Human Domain was slowly collapsing somewhere far above them.

The Night Garden was severely overpopulated at the moment, and although everyone lived in cramped conditions, there was enough food for everyone. There were also seven Saints aboard: Jet, Nightwalker, Bloodwave, Aether, Naeve, Tyris, and Roan. So even if they ran out of some supplies, they could bring more from the waking world.
The problem lay somewhere else.
Taking another bite of the fruit, Jet narrowed her eyes as she scrutinized the movement of people on the main decks of the Night Garden.

Everything seemed in order. However, something felt off to her eyes. There were invisible undercurrents and signs of internal strife that weren't that difficult to notice, but there was something else, too.

She knew that most of the mundane population of her Citadel had already succumbed to the plague of Asterion. The loyalty of the Awakened warriors was questionable, well. There were plenty of ways to confirm that someone still belonged to the Longing Domain, but Jet doubted that any of them were truly trustworthy.
In fact, she was willing to bet that even if there was no reasonable way to fool the tests, the Dreamspawn had long found a way to do just that.

Everyone acted like they used to, but at the same time, many people raised her suspicion. So, Jet and the other Saints, as well as the Ascended officers under their command, had been carefully positioning the troops they deemed trustworthy — or at least hoped that they were — near the vital elements of the living ship's ecosystem.

The loyalists and the turncoats... the believers and the apostates... aboard the Night Garden were constantly getting into small conflicts, and the soldiers under Jet's command were busy making sure that things did not get out of hand.
The living ship was like a powder keg, and days passed in the atmosphere of distrust and tension.
It felt like a mutiny was brewing.
Jet chewed on the golden fruit calmly.

If the mundane population of the Night Garden rebelled... even if the Awakened soldiers joined them, they would not be able to gain control of the living ship. That was simply because they would not be able to defeat the seven Saints who guarded it. However, Jet did not suspect only the mundane people and the Awakened. She was an equal opportunity offender, so she did not fully trust her Ascended officers, either, and was watching the other six Saints — even Nightwalker — with a wary eye, too.
There were definitely questionable things about them. There were more and more strange discrepancies Jet noticed every day... if it wasn't all in her head.
They were probably having the same thoughts about her.

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2822.txt
content: Yesterday, Jet had shared a bottle of strong spirits with Nightwalker. The conversation was relaxed, but inevitably turned to their current predicament.

“The ship absorbs an ocean of essence from the divine flame, but climbing up to the surface will be difficult. We can train young Tyris and her husband to steer the ship, but even with six Saints working in shifts, we'll run ourselves ragged."
She gave him a curious glance.
“What are you getting at?"
Nightwalker looked her up and down, then grinned.

“Well, your pool of essence is several times deeper than that of any of us. Plus, you're the captain. It seems peculiar that you are not standing at the helm, no? Won't you take at least a shift or two?"

Jet studied him with a relaxed smile. Nothing of what he said was wrong, but she had removed herself from the rotation for a reason. When the Saints of Night steered the Night Garden through the air, all they had to do after a shift was spend a few days replenishing their essence. Jet could not do the same, though — instead, she would have to hunt a significant number of Nightmare Creatures to refill her shattered core. Which was not a problem, usually... however, she would be vulnerable until she did. If someone wanted to eliminate her, that would be the perfect moment to attack.
Was Nightwalker really asking her to take the helm because he wanted to spare the rest of the Saints some exhaustion, or was there a more sinister intent behind his request?
She took a sip of alcohol and shrugged, her smile remaining the same.
“I'll think about it”
A day before that, Naeve had been making a routine report to her.

“There were a total of twenty-four violent altercations between the civilians yesterday. Fifteen connected to the strife between the Domains, the rest due to mundane reasons. Bruises and lacerations, nothing serious. The troublemakers were detained to let them cool down."
He checked his notes.

“The food stores are holding up well. A few are depleted, but we are almost ready to harvest the innermost fields. That should cover the difference. All in all, our surplus seems sufficient to feed the additional population. There's nothing else to report, really."
Jet nodded.
"Good."
She glanced at him briefly, then asked in a neutral tone.
"Oh, by the way. Did you change the guard shifts in the Mainmast Pagoda a few days back?"
Naeve remained silent for a moment, checking his notes again. After shuffling through several pages, he nodded.
“Yes, there was a minor change."
Jet raised an eyebrow.
"Why?"
He shrugged.
“A few soldiers were seen fraternizing with the turncoats, so I had to replace them. That, in turn, required pushing the shift change timing a bit."
Jet remained silent.
She wouldn't call changing security personnel a minor issue. Even if it was, why hadn't Naeve informed her?
Was it peculiar, or was she just being paranoid?
She smiled, then glanced away and nodded.
"Okay."
At some point between these two conversations, Jet was showing Sky Tide around the ship. They talked as they moved between the decks.
"Anyway. I hear that your daughter has run off to challenge a Nightmare?"
Tyris pursed her lips.
"She did.”
Jet studied her briefly, then chuckled quietly.
“Like father, like daughter. The apple does not fall far from the tree, huh?"
Tyris sighed and fell silent. Eventually, though, she asked:
“Is that where our Sovereign is? Has she run off to challenge the Fifth Nightmare?"
Jet spared her another look. The champions of the Human Domain were naturally curious about where Changing Star was, considering that the world seemed to be falling apart at an abominable speed.
“As I said before, I do not know."

She wasn't lying. Jet herself did not know where Nephis and Sunny had disappeared to, only that they had gone to complete a vital mission. Even Cassie did not seem to know, despite the fact that she had been the one to inform them about that development.
Tyris frowned.
“Do you really not know, or are you only saying that you don't know?"
Jet turned to look at her.
It was natural for Sky Tide to want to know where the Supreme she had sworn to serve was. However... that was the third time she had asked Jet, despite receiving the same answer the first two times.
Was she really asking out of concern... or was she asking because someone else really, really wanted to know where his opponents were?
"I really don't know."
Tyris did not ask her the same question again, but would stare at her strangely from time to time after that.
All of it was quite distressing. The suspicion, the ambiguity, the waiting... it was honestly a bit tiring.
Jet almost wished that, if something were to happen, it would happen already.
‘I wonder... can I defeat all six, if things devolve into bloodshed?’
She exhaled slowly.

Tyris and Roan had the terrain advantage here, in the Sky Below. Naeve and Bloodwave were at a disadvantage instead. Aether... she wasn't sure how his Aspect and the divine flames influenced each other, so it was hard to tell.
Nightwalker was an old monster who was utterly lethal in any environment, as well as the one she was most wary of.
Still...
If Jet had to bet, she would bet on herself. If they were to attack her, she'd probably kill at least most of them.
Possibly even all of them.
She really wished they wouldn't, though. Chances were, they were not going to. There were easier ways to defeat her than a battle, after all.

There was an entire ship of people under her command, and each of those people could become a hostage. What was she going to do if the Saint at the helm of the Night Garden threatened to drop it into the ocean of divine flames unless she surrendered?
What would she do if they forced her to calculate the value of her own life against the unknown number of lives she was meant to protect?
The golden fruits were usually sweet, but today, the one she had plucked tasted bitter.
Jet sighed.
“Life is indeed full of surprises. But, strangely... I miss the days when I had nothing to lose..."

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2823.txt
content: Days passed while the Night Garden drifted through the vast emptiness of the Sky Below.

Far above, the King of Nothing had already conquered the Glass Hell and the Chained Isles. Now, his forces were invading Godgrave and the Moonriver Plains, as well as moving south, toward the distant Black Mountains.

The Black Mountains had served as the boundary of the lands tamed by humanity once, decades ago. Then, Anvil and Madoc of Valor led their Knights on a conquest crusade to subjugate the regions of the Dream Realm laying further north, eventually reaching all the way to the Hollow Mountains.
Now, Mordret was taking the lands they had conquered back from humanity. It was as if he were erasing everything his father had achieved, one feat at a time.
‘I guess it's a little poetic... in a morbidly sinister kind of way.'
Jet was descending toward the runic hall of the Mainmast Pagoda.
There, Nightwalker was currently at the helm of the living ship. The two of them had been busy for the last few days, working closely together to develop an alternative way of escaping the Sky Below.

The simplest way was to ascend the same way they had descended — slowly and arduously, pouring oceans of essence into the Night Garden. The obvious drawbacks of this method were that it would exhaust the Saints, as well as that they would escape the abyss in the middle of the Chained Isles, which currently belonged to Mordret.
The more difficult way was to use one of the living ship's Components to open a portal to somewhere else.

Opening spatial rifts and moving through them was one of the abilities Night Garden had gained after visiting the Eternal City. The problem was that, like many of its other Continents, it was not really meant to be used by a Saint. Jet could open a portal, but only as far as her eyes could see — which was largely meaningless, since the living ship could breach that gap in no time all by itself.

To truly make use of that function of the living ship, they had to master a different one first — a function that had to do with navigation. However, no matter how much time they spent on studying it, the puzzle still remained unsolved.
In the end, Jet could not open a portal to guide the Night Garden away from the Sky Below and the Chained Isles — she wasn't a good enough navigator for that.
However... there was Nightwalker.

He not only possessed the lineage of Storm God, the god of travel and guidance, but also had an Aspect Ability that allowed him to always know where to go in order to get to where he wanted to arrive.

So, in theory, Nightwalker did not even need to master the navigation tools of the Night Garden. As long as they could properly integrate his Aspect into the sorcery of the living ship, he would probably be able to open a portal to almost anywhere without having to master a different function of the ship.
That was what Jet and Nightwalker had been working on ever since the Night Garden plunged into the Sky Below, and by now, it felt like they were nearing a turning point.
All the while, the plague of Asterion was ravaging the population of the living ship, and paranoia was slowly taking over the Saints who were meant to protect it.
Jet entered the runic hall and nodded at Nightwalker, who was standing in the circle.
"How is it?"
He gave her an apologetic smile.
“Sorry. I haven't been able to make any progress since yesterday."
Jet remained silent for a few moments, looking at him.
Then, she smiled as well.
“Okay."
‘Wasn't he saying that he could almost feel the connection yesterday?’
She chose not to comment.
Instead, she turned to the other two people present in the runic hall. They were Naeve and Aether, discussing something quietly among themselves.
“And what are you two doing here?"
Distracted from their conversation, they greeted her warmly.
“Oh, we are just waiting for my uncle."
Naeve seemed inpatient.

In recent days, the Saints under her command had taken it upon themselves to take turns travelling to the waking world. There, they gathered information about the current situation, as well as procured various supplies that the ship needed. Aether looked at Naeve, then politely offered a more detailed explanation:

“He is supposed to bring a civil engineer who can help us deal with the excess heat. The ship is overcrowded, and with all those flames below us, people are quite uncomfortable. So, we hope to quite literally cool them off. If we do, perhaps the amount of problems they create will lessen."
Heat waves had always been known to increase the crime rate. The living ship was already like a powder keg, so their motive seemed reasonable.
At least at first glance... as long as one did not think too hard about it.
Jet studied them quietly.
"Good thinking."
The compliment was hollow.
At that moment, there was the sound of steps, and two more Saints suddenly appeared in the runic hall. They were Tyris and Roan, looking around with reserved curiosity.
Jet spared them a confused look.
“And why are you here?"
Roan flashed her a charismatic smile.
"Nightwalker insisted that we learn how to steer this ship, in case we need to help out with supplying it with essence. So, we are here for a lesson."
Nightwalker waved from the runic circle.
“Right, right! Just wait for me quietly. You can observe what I do in the meantime."
Jet remained silent.

It made sense that Nightwalker would want to teach Tyris and Roan how to control the ship. They indeed needed more hands, considering how much essence the Night Garden would consume as it left the sea of flames behind.
However...
Jet looked around carefully.
Nightwalker was in front of her. Naeve and Aether were flanking her. Tyris and Roan, meanwhile, were still standing at the entrance.
Cutting off her path of escape.
Was it a coincidence, or a trap?
Jet suddenly felt a cold shiver run down his spine.
And was Bloodwave really bridging in a civil engineer?
Just as she thought that, two figures appeared in the grand emptiness of the runic hall.
One of them was the familiar tall Saint of the House of Night.
The other...
Had golden eyes.

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2824.txt
content: Just a few moments before Bloodwave and the uninvited guest appeared in the runic hall of the Night Garden, Jet, who had been looking at her fellow Saints with a wary expression, smiled suddenly.
She turned to Nightwalker.
"You know what? I think I'll take that shift, after all. You look exhausted."
He raised an eyebrow.
"Oh, really?"
Jet was already stepping into the runic circle.
She maintained an easy smile as a cold shiver ran down her spine.
"What's this? You don't seem happy at all. After complaining so much..."
She patted him on the shoulder and gently pushed him out of the circle. Nightwalker seemed a little reluctant, but he did not have a reason to refuse, so he left without saying anything.
By then, the silhouettes of two people were already forming in the air.

As the captain of the Night Garden, Jet was always connected to it. However, that connection was usually subtle and vague, existing in the deep periphery of her subconscious mind. It was only when she stepped into the runic circle that the connection opened fully, allowing her to share the feelings of the living ship and exert precise control over it.
Now, too, she suddenly both remained herself and became a vast presence that bathed in the nurturing heat of divine flames as her body sheltered and nurtured millions of living beings in turn.
Time seemed to slow down for a moment. Jet could only see the vague silhouettes of Bloodwave and the civil engineer he was supposed to bring as they were manifested in the Dream Realm.

However, the Night Garden felt their arrival far more deeply. One was familiar and caring. The other was a stranger, his presence as vast as an ocean and full of endless, insatiable hunger... and kinship, as well.
The stranger bore the scent of Heart God and its sacred forest.
Jet's smile faltered.
‘A civil engineer, my foot.'

Before she could react, Bloodwave and the man he carried across the realm boundary fully materialized on the floor of the runic hall. The supposed engineer was tall and imposing, looking at her with two radiant golden eyes.
He was none other than Supreme Asterion, the Dreamspawn, in the flesh.
Jet felt her blood running cold.
She had expected an ambush... but she had not expected that.
As Asterion looked around the bridge of the Night Garden with curiosity, the other Saints — Naeve, Aether, Bloodwave, Tyris, Roan, and even Nightwalker — bowed.
“Welcome, my lord."
The corner of Jet's mouth twitched as she spared Nightwalker a dark look.
Him too? After all the torment and horror he had endured because of the Dreamspawn?
...Or had he been a thrall of the Dreamspawn this whole time?
‘No, it couldn't be.'
Jet's mood soured.
Still, she mentally patted herself on the back. Her distrust of the other Saints had turned out to be justified — if there was one thing that had never failed her in this life, it was paranoia.
Finally, Asterion's gaze settled on Jet. She met it steadily, trying her best not to appear rattled.
He smiled.
“Ah, Soul Reaper Jet. What a charming nickname. It is a pleasure to meet you... oh, but you don't seem to be surprised to see me at all?"
Jet remained silent for a few moments, then forced on a faint smile of her own.
“On the contrary. I am quite shocked."
She glanced at the Saints that were scattered around the runic hall, surrounding her, and shrugged.

“I was expecting that the six of them would try to subdue me with an unexpected attack. Never in my wildest dreams did I imagine that Lord Asterion himself would come to deal with this humble Transcendent."
He chuckled.
"A humble Transcendent? Please, don't sell yourself short."
Asterion studied Jet with a hint of amusement in his golden eyes.

"I've met all kinds of people, you know? Powerful people, cunning people, great masters of warfare and the like... even people whose main virtue was that they were inexplicably lucky, which made them more dangerous than all the rest. And among all those people, you'd still rank as one of the most impressive, Miss Jet."
Jet scoffed.
“What is this, flattery?"
He shrugged.

"No, no... I'm simply stating the facts. Everyone has a weakness, you see. But not all weaknesses can be easily exploited. Powerful people, cunning people, lucky people, and great masters of all kinds — dealing with them poses no problem at all. To tell you the truth, I wasn't planning to act personally at this stage, since delegating tasks to my subordinates was more than sufficient."
Asterion sighed.

"But no matter how much I tried, I couldn't see a way for them to deal with Soul Reaper Jet. Not without sacrificing more talents than I am willing to sacrifice, at least, or without destroying you instead of making you mine. It is quite an accomplishment, really... I can't believe that we all overlooked such a gem, all those years ago."
He looked at Jet and smiled brightly.
“So, I had to come and deal with you myself. There are only two Transcendents who managed to make me act personally, by the way. Well done."
Jet studied him with a concerned expression.
"...What an honor."
Asterion laughed.
“I feel a note of sarcasm in your voice, but actually, it is indeed an honor. You should feel proud."
Jet studied him for a few moments, then sighed.
“You know, people like me — those who grew up in the outskirts — don't like the word honor that much. In fact, one might say that we can't stand it."
He raised an eyebrow, looking at her with curiosity.
“The outskirts? I am sorry, but I am not familiar with the term. Is that something from your world?"
A grin slowly bloomed on Jet's lips.

"Right. You're not from Earth, so you might not know. Yes, it's a term from the waking world. The outskirts are where the non-citizens lived, and they had a peculiar culture. People from the outskirts — those who live long enough to escape them, at least — are known to be distrustful, vicious, cynical, and tenacious. They often share common traits, as well."
She took a deep breath and glanced around the runic hall, chillingly evaluating the Sovereign and the six Saints that surrounded her.
“Like, for example..."
The runes of the circle shimmered faintly, and a subtle change spread across the hall. Jet smiled darkly.
“They tend to never enter a room without an exit strategy..."

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2825.txt
content: A while back, Jet had asked herself if she would be able to kill the six Saints aboard the Night Garden if they were to conspire against her.
She had also asked herself what she would do if the entire population of the living ship was taken hostage or turned on her.

The answer to the first question was... yes, probably. She could not be sure, but deep down, she knew that they would not survive a battle against her. Her Aspect was simply too lethal, and the gap in physical prowess between her and the rest of the Saints was simply too vast — in that regard, she was closer to a Supreme than to a Transcendent.

Besides all that... she was simply a killer. That innate quality was hard to define or measure, but those who knew its fatal scent could easily distinguish between those who possessed it and those who did not. Needless to say, the former was many times more dangerous.
It was just that slaying six powerful Saints would not help anyone. Whether she managed to kill them or not, humanity would suffer a grievous loss — so, what was the point in trying?
As for the second question, Jet was honestly not sure. She did, however, know that if things ever came to that, the battle would have already been lost.

And while she would have fought for the cause of her people to the last drop of blood if there was a smallest possibility of victory, only a fool would continue to bleed themselves dry in a battle that had already ended in defeat.

Saving herself to fight another day was an option, too, even if it meant surrendering the Night Garden and millions of souls to the enemy. After all, she would not be able to snatch them from Asterion's grasp even if she stayed.
So, Jet had been hoping for the best while preparing for the worst possible outcome in secret.
She was ready to escape instead of fighting the six Saints.
Sadly... she had really not expected that Asterion would come for her personally. His presence here complicated things. A lot. Looking at Jet, Asterion chuckled.
"It does complicate things, doesn't it?"
Jet's expression darkened.
She remained silent for a few seconds, then said in a disgruntled tone:
“It's impolite to read a lady's private thoughts."
Asterion simply shrugged.

"It's also impolite to cast all your thoughts and emotions outward as if you were screaming at the top of your lungs — constantly, incessantly, for everyone to hear. Noise, noise, noise... you people are noisy enough to drive a person mad. It's deafening. I spent a long time learning how to stay sane in the endless cacophony of your souls."
His lips twisted into a friendly smile.

"So, forgive me for taking a peek at your plans of escape. Granted, you've been cunning enough to hide the details of how exactly you were going to run away even from your own mind... but that shouldn't matter too much."
The friendly smile suddenly seemed menacing.
"Should it, Miss Jet?"
Jet blinked, having caught herself staring into his golden eyes.
"Wait..."

What... what had she been thinking about? Jet knew that she had prepared an escape strategy. However, no matter how much she tried to remember what it was, she couldn't. It was as if there was an empty space in her mind where her plan had been... or possibly a tall wall that prevented her from accessing these thoughts.
She tried to hide her fear and glanced at Asterion with the same concerned expression.
"That's a neat trick. However, do you really think that you can keep me here against my will? I am quite good at improvising as well, you know."
Asterion laughed.
"I know. But why would you want to leave?"
Jet blinked again, confused.
Indeed. Why would she want to leave?
She vaguely remembered wanting to escape, but could not comprehend why. This was her Citadel, after all. Her people were here; her home was here, too, as well as her beautiful garden.
There seemed to be no reason to leave the Night Garden, and every reason to stay.
Asterion sighed in satisfaction.
"There. That's better"
Jet glanced at him in a daze.
‘No, wait. Something is not right here.'
It felt like she had forgotten something.
But what was it? She couldn't put her finger on it.
The other Saints moved, slowly getting closer to the runic circle. Asterion took a step forward, as well, his golden eyes holding her gaze.
"Now, let us..."
But before he could finish the sentence, something strange happened.
Instead of landing on the wooden floor, his foot seemed to sink into it, making Asterion stumble. An expression of surprise appeared on his face, replaced a moment later by a pained grimace.
"Miss Jet. Is your ship... trying to eat me, by any chance?"
Asterion, Nightwalker, and the rest of the Saints were slowly sinking into the floor, as if it were quicksand instead of solid wood. Watching that, Jet frowned thoughtfully. Then, she smiled.
“Ah! I remember now. The plan I improvised."
Asterion stared at her for a moment, then snarled.

He moved, and the wooden floor around him exploded into splinters. At the same time, Nightwalker — who had been standing closest to Jet — flicked his wrist, sending an ominously gleaming throwing knife at her. The enchanted knife hit Jet in the chest... and passed through her, as if flying through mist.
She gave him a bitter look.
“Bastard. We'll talk about it later..."
In the next moment, Asterion was standing near her, his hand stretching forward to grab her neck.
But before he could...
Jet vanished without a trace, as if she had never been there.
At the same time, she appeared far below, on the main deck of the Night Garden. Jet lost her balance, fell into a roll, and slid a few meters before jumping to her feet.
All around her, hundreds of people — thousands, possibly — were all staring at her with ominous expressions. Some of them were mundane, some were Awakened, and more than a few were Masters.
All of them, however, seemed to have been enthralled by the Dreamspawn already.
'I'm surrounded by zombies...'
No. Was she the zombie?
Now that Jet was far away from the Dreamspawn, her mind seemed to have been freed from the hex. So, she could remember what she had been planning to do, and why.
‘Escape, huh...’
It was easier said than done when a demented Sovereign was hot on her heels.

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2826.txt
content: Jet realized that all her plans had become meaningless the moment she felt Asterion's presence through the Night Garden. Dealing with six powerful Saints was already difficult enough. Dealing with six Saints and a damn Supreme on top of that was... well above her paygrade. Now that Asterion boarded the Night Garden, executing her escape would be all but impossible, and on top of that, his Aspect granted him frightening powers.

The second he appeared in the runic hall, he would be able to read her mind and learn how exactly she was planning to get away. Even worse, he would be able to manipulate her very thoughts, twisting them and turning her own mind against her.
Jet prided herself on being paranoid, but now, she couldn't even trust herself.

She had to improvise an entirely new plan of escape in the brief moment between the vague silhouettes of Bloodwave and Asterion appearing in the runic hall and them fully crossing the realm boundary, manifesting in the Dream Realm — not only that, but she had to make that plan impervious to the powers of the malevolent Sovereign, somehow.
So, she did just that.

By the time Asterion laid eyes on her, the new plan had already been put in place. Jet also made an effort to focus all her thoughts on the old one, forbidding herself from thinking about anything else.
It seemed to be working, for now.

She had not been certain that it would. Her new plan was quite simple, but also innately devious in its simplicity. It relied on a single proposition — that while Asterion could manipulate and deceive her own mind, even he could not manipulate the vast, slumbering mind of the Night Garden.
The Night Garden was not just any ship, after all. It was a living ship, and while it did not quite possess a consciousness in the way people understood the concept, it was not truly mindless either.
So, in that brief moment before Asterion appeared in the runic hall, Jet gave the Night Garden a simple command.
Protect its captain.

That was why the Dreamspawn and the Saints he had subjugated suddenly started to sink into the floor. The Mainmast Pagoda and the floor of the runic hall were carved from the same wood as the hull of the living ship — the Night Garden had never absorbed and digested its passengers, but not because it could not.
Rather, it was because it had been damaged in the past, and there was no reason to consume the beings it was meant to shelter and nurture anyway.

Jet doubted that Asterion and the Saints would be seriously harmed by that simple defensive measure, but they had at least been slowed down at a decisive moment. Next, the Night Garden sent her away from the runic hall, where the danger was, to its main deck. Jet suspected that it had opened a miniature portal between the two — just like it could open a vast rift in space to travel from one place to another.
‘So... something like that was possible, too.'

The sudden break of the connection between Jet and the living ship was a bit jarring, but she swiftly regained her senses. She was standing on one of the outer decks of the Night Garden now, surrounded from all sides by hostile thralls of the Dreamspawn. Asterion was already aware of her current location, no doubt. He would escape the runic hall in a matter of moments, so...
She needed to run.
There was nowhere to run in the Sky Below, really. The Night Garden was surrounded by a vast emptiness from all sides — except for one, where a sea of divine flames burned in the darkness.
However, Jet was a Saint, and Saints could always escape across the realm boundary to a different world.
It was just that pulling on one's tether required time and concentration.
Jet had little time and could not afford to focus entirely on her tether. Still, she had to manage it, somehow.

Just when a small figure exploded from the arched portals of the runic hall far above, at the top of the Mainmast Pagoda, she lunged into a run. She had to buy herself more time, and there was only one way to do it. From where she stood, only a few hundred meters separated her from the edge of the Night Garden, beyond which lay the empty dark void — a distance that a Saint could breach in the blink of an eye. When people moved to bar her way, Jet simply turned into a mist and flowed past them, rushing toward the railing.
However, bizarrely, it took her several seconds just to move a few dozen steps.
Jet cursed.
‘Nightwalker, that old lecher!’
The legendary Saint was twisting space to increase the distance between her and the dark void. He couldn't stop her entirely, but he could slow her down.
Somewhere above her, two flying figures dove out of the towering pagoda following the first — they were Tyris and Roan, without a doubt.
Behind her, meanwhile, Asterion landed on the deck of the Night Garden like a meteor.
'Damn it.'

Jet poured a roaring torrent of essence into her muscles. Her dead body was special, as it could absorb far more essence than the flesh of the living could — so, she could move faster than almost any Saint, streaking across the deck like lightning.

But not as swift as Asterion was moving as he lunged after her. The Dreamspawn did not even seem to run; he simply blinked out of existence at one spot and appeared in the next — in the blink of an eye, he was already upon her.

It was then that the Night Garden suddenly tilted, throwing Asterion off balance while simultaneously pushing Jet toward her goal. She leaped onto a colossal cannon that stood in front of the railing, then jumped into the empty space below.
An arrow pierced her shoulder at the last moment, wounding her and making her bleed.
Jet was spun around as she fell, witnessing the radiant sight of the countless lanterns that illuminated the Night Garden for the last time.
‘Goodbye, my wondrous ship. Thank you. I hope we'll meet again...'
Then, she plummeted down, into the depths of the Sky Below — and the fierce radiance of the ocean of flame that burned beneath her.
‘This... is going to hurt.'
Watching two enormous figures — a magnificent bird of prey and a winged lion — diving to catch her, Jet focused on her tether.
She fell, and fell, and fell... as she did, the blinding radiance of divine flames grew more intense around her.
The heat was suffocating at first.
Then, when Jet crossed the invisible barrier of the living ship's aegis, it became lethal. Her armor ignited, and a terrible pain flooded her mind.
The talons of the enormous thunderbird were moments away from snatching her. But in the end, failed to reach her.
Burning, Jet fell into the sea of flames.
‘l wonder..."

A moment later, she rolled across cold concrete in a cloud of smoke and crashed into a cracked wall, shattering it entirely. As pieces of concrete and dust fell on her from above, Jet coughed and let out a pained groan.
‘...am I the first Saint to escape from a Supreme?"
No, come to think of it, Cassie had escaped too. Asterion seemed to have terrible luck with catching Saints.
Jet, on the other hand, had been quite lucky. She had returned to the waking world.

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2827.txt
content: Even after escaping the Dream Realm, Jet was under no illusion that she was safe now.

In fact, she was confident that since Asterion had come to capture her, he would have prepared an ambush in the government stronghold where she kept her tether, as well. He had already proven himself capable of being quite thorough during the attack on Cassie.

That was why she had secretly moved her tether to an undisclosed location weeks ago, with no one being the wiser. Not even the other members of Changing Star's cohort knew where she was tethered now, so there was little chance of the Dreamspawn tracing her current location quickly.
He would find her eventually, though, so Jet had little time to waste.
Taking a deep breath, she gritted her teeth and sat up.

Her armor had been burned to a crisp and was already collapsing into a rain of dancing sparks. Beneath it, the snow-white smoothness of her skin was interspersed with gruesome burns. A good portion of her body was scorched and blackened, creating a ghastly sight.
Jet exhaled a small cloud of smoke and smiled darkly.
At least she was alive.
Well... maybe not quite alive. But definitely in one piece.
[Close your eyes, Jet.]
Hearing Cassie's voice in her mind, Jet lingered for a moment and then did as she had been told.
[Why?]
There was a brief silence.
[I cannot be sure that the Dreamspawn has no access to my mind. So, it would be best if you don't show me where you are. After this conversation is over, I will erase my mark and cut our connection.]
Jet let out a heavy sigh.

She briefly enjoyed the feeling of cold air caressing her burns, and then spoke quietly: “l assume you already know what happened. The Night Garden... is lost. And that Nightmare Creature seemed to have grown strong enough to easily sway Saints. Something changed."

Dar of the Maharana clan was the first Transcendent to fall under the Dreamspawn's spell... or so they had thought. However, the plague had swallowed Red Hill too suddenly and too rapidly, leaving them suspicious. It was difficult to investigate what had happened because Mordret left no witnesses, but there were breadcrumbs to be found in the last messages of Saint Dar.

The people of Red Hill had suffered from insomnia caused by a persistent plague of nightmares. This clue led Nephis and her companions to Saint Thane, the Dream Merchant... who had been developing a new version of Dreamspace to serve as a way for distant Citadels of humanity to communicate with each other.
Jet, naturally, was very familiar with the Dream Merchant, who had once been one of the two government Saints alongside Wake of Ruin.

It was unclear when Asterion had enthralled Saint Thane. What was clear, however, was that the unseen damage caused to the Human Domain by the infected dreams was grave. It had accelerated the spread of the plague, making countless people across the Dream Realm far more susceptible to it. Red Hill had been targeted especially severely. Much worse, the Dream Merchant had remained a subject of the Longing Domain until the very end, proving that Asterion was capable of far more than simply making his thralls blindly loyal to his cause.
After that discovery, Nephis and her allies could not even tell how many Saints had already fallen under Asterion's hex.
But, still...

Enthralling all six Saints aboard the Night Garden — including someone as stalwart as Tyris or as exceptionally predisposed to loathing the Dreamspawn as Nightwalker — was too difficult a feat. It had all happened too fast.
Cassie seemed to agree.
[It is most likely because of what happened at the Lake of Tears. The plague seems to have reached critical mass there. Now, it's... unstoppable.]

Jet was sitting on the cold concrete with her eyes closed, as if meditating. Her body was like a morbidly beautiful painting, white and black hues accentuated by streaks of vibrant red. Her expression was somber.
She remained silent for a while, then said in an even tone:
"Since the Night Garden has fallen... Bastion and Ravenheart will follow soon."
Her voice sounded hoarse in the cold darkness.
Cassie's answer was delayed by a few seconds.
[It seems that way. In fact...]
She paused for a moment, and then added calmly:
[I lost track of the Night Garden. So, an attack on the Ivory Tower seems imminent as well.]
A dark grimace contorted Jet's face.
“So... what am I supposed to do now? Every countermeasure I can think of is bound to end up being an exercise in futility."
Cassie remained silent for a while before responding.
[Put your faith in Nephis and Sunny. Hide and survive until they return.]
Jet chuckled.
"I cannot really lie low, Cassie. If I do, my essence will run out, and I'll die. No matter where I'll go, there needs to be something for me to kill there."
Cassie had to know it, as well.
[Escape NQSC for now. It is not safe there anymore... I'll provide an escape route. Once you are not in immediate danger, you can think of a way to satiate your Flaw.]
Receiving the instructions on where to go, Jet smiled bitterly.
“How ironic. But I like it, actually... that’s the last place they will look, considering the circumstances."
She fell silent for a moment, then asked: “What about you?"
Cassie's response sounded confident, but she could sense a hint of tension in it.
[If worst comes to worst... I have a few escape routes prepared, as well. Hopefully, though, I won't have to use them.]
Jet exhaled slowly.
For a few seconds, there was nothing but silence in her mind.
"I guess you'll be cutting the connection between us now, then?"
Cassie responded after a short pause:
[Yes. There is nothing left to say.]
Jet sighed again.
"Take care of yourself, Cassie."
Just before she felt a familiar presence disappear from her mind, she suddenly asked:
“Wait... Nephis and the Lord of Shadows. Where are they, really?"
There was a moment of silence, and then Cassie chuckled.
[I don't know.]
Jet scoffed.
[I don't know, because I erased my own memories of having known where they went. So, even if the Dreamspawn captures me, he won't find out.]
She fell silent for a moment.
[Take care of yourself as well, Jet.]
With that, Cassie was gone.
Jet had never really known when their seer was sharing her senses, but now, she suddenly felt lonely in her own mind.
A moment later, her icy blue eyes gleamed in the darkness.
“Right. Let's escape NQSC, then..."

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2828.txt
content: Now that most residents of the outskirts left for the Dream Realm, the vast human hives that had teemed with life and desperation stood mostly empty. There were still people living there, though — even if some dormitory towers only had a few dozen inhabitants instead of tens of thousands.
A family that had once sheltered in a single residential cell now occupied an entire block. Even then, the rest of the floor was empty.

In the dim twilight of the early dawn, a gaunt man emerged into a corridor and leaned on the wall, contorted in a violent coughing fit. His wife was already busy boiling water to hydrate packs of synthpaste for the kids — however, he had to put on his work uniform and depart for an early shift. Wiping his lips, he stumbled toward the laundry room.
A few seconds later, however, he froze and looked ahead in horror.
Out there in front of him, the claustrophobic expanse of the dormitory tower was drowning in cold mist, and for a brief moment, he saw a ghostly silhouette moving through it.
Flesh as pale as that of a corpse, harrowing burns that seeped with scarlet blood... Screaming, the man staggered away.
"G—ghost! Ghost!"
Turning around, he ran as if his life depended on it.
A few moments after the man fled, a pale arm extended from the mist toward the clothesline.
‘It's like I came full circle.'

Jet had grown up in a dormitory hive very much like this one, surrounded by gaunt people with waxy skin who were prone to coughing fits. She had been sickly and neglected, as well, wearing clothes that were threadbare and did not fit her frail figure.
Some clothes were better than no clothes, though.

Putting on a pair of worn pants, a shabby shirt, and a ragged coat, she grimaced when the coarse fabric scraped against her burns and turned into mist. Soon, she was on the ground floor of the dormitory tower, hiding in the shadows of the back entrance awning and looking up.
Out there in the pale sky above NQSC, a storm of crows was painting mesmerizing patterns among clouds as it flowed in a vast murmuration.
Her expression darkened.
‘Old man...’

Jet could tell a lot about what was going on in the city just from how it sounded. On this hazy morning, there were sounds of armored PTVs rolling on the asphalt, sirens howling in the distance, and the hum of sharp voices calling out to each other, muffled by the distance.

These particular noises were different from the usual clamor of the city, as well as from the panicked urgency of an opening Gate. As someone who had been the government's enforcer for many years, Jet knew the nature of this rough melody too well.
This was the sound of a large-scale manhunt.
Things like that happened rarely, since the government preferred to solve most issues quietly. However, she had led a few of such manhunts herself.
Today, Jet was quite sure that the person being hunted... was her.
Finding herself on the other side of law enforcement felt strange.
She traced the murmurations of the crows for a few moments, then looked away so that Wake of Ruin did not sense her gaze.
‘Is he enthralled, too?'

Even if Jet did not want to believe it, she had an ominous feeling that the person in charge of hunting her down was none other than Old Cor. There were too many signs that pointed at him... and even if there weren't, preparing for the worst had never failed her in the past. The world had nothing but the worst in store for the people born in the era of the Nightmare Spell, after all.
Getting to her destination unnoticed while Wake of Ruin was watching the city from high above would be difficult.
Muttering a quiet curse, Jet hid deeper in the shadows and collapsed into a torrent of mist.

She traveled into the depths of the city while hiding her presence as best she could. When it was possible, she moved underground. When the patrolling Awakened got too close, she avoided them. When a barrier stood in her way, she passed through it like a wraith.

Catching a ghost in the cauldron of human souls as vast as NQSC was not easy, even for the powerful machinery of the government and its oldest champion, Wake of Ruin. Especially if that ghost knew every gear of that complicated machine by heart.
That said... the city was different from how it had been the last time she visited.

It was in the throes of internal conflict. The streets were oddly desolate. Here and there, burning vehicles were scattered on the roads. Groups of soldiers were moving between them, wearing grim expressions. Many buildings were gaping with broken windows, pieces of furniture and debris spilling on the pavements.
In some places, the ground was smeared with blood.
The noise of the city was wrong and unhealthy, as if NQSC was being consumed by fever.
‘It's all going south.'
The sun had already risen by the time Jet made it to where Cassie had sent her.

In front of her was the heavily fortified government compound in the heart of the city — the very place where Jet had been anchored before secretly moving her tether to the desolate ruin in the outskirts.

She had gone to all the trouble to escape it, so naturally, the Dreamspawn and his servants would not expect that she would immediately return here. In that sense, her destination alone had helped her remain unnoticed.

The government compound was in disarray. The gates were open, and there were no guards posted outside. Smoke was billowing from the windows of several levels... which meant that the automatic defense systems had either malfunctioned or were disabled, failing to execute a lockdown protocol. Most tellingly of all, there were muffled sounds of gunshots and heavy impacts resounding from inside.
Jet assumed her human shape and walked across the broken asphalt barefoot, her ragged coat fluttering in the wind.
Her expression was unamused.
“I leave for a bit, and the whole place is a mess..."
Stepping over the unconscious body of an Awakened soldier, she entered the compound.

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2829.txt
content: The familiar lobby of the government compound was uncharacteristically empty... except for the corpses.
The walls were cracked and riddled with holes. The furniture was destroyed. The floor and the ceiling were splattered with blood.

Jet regarded the scene of devastation with a distant expression. It seemed like there had been a firefight here recently... mundane soldiers had clashed with each other at first, and were then wiped out by the Awakened. The battle had moved on deeper into the building since then.

Judging by the sounds and the mild tremors running through the floor, only sporadic skirmishes were still happening on the upper floors of the compound, while most of the violence was happening underground.
In all the years since its establishment, the government complex in NQSC had never been breached — yet now, it seemed to have fallen.
However, the compound had not been attacked from outside. The scene of the lobby told Jet that much... it had been compromised from within.

The thralls of Asterion who had been hidden among the government personnel must have finally turned on those of their comrades who still remained loyal to the Human Domain. The Dreamspawn was tired of waiting and had the means to take control, since most of humanity had already fallen to his plague... so, he gave his servants a command to finish the job forcefully. Frowning in displeasure, Jet made her way into the depths of the compound. She grimaced as she walked, the pain of her burns radiating across her body like heat. Her wounds were painful, but not life-threatening. Even if they were, Jet was not quite alive, so they would not have threatened her existence. Still, she wished that Nephis would heal them, already... sadly, Nephis could not heal every minor scrape. If she did, she would have been overcome by an endless, relentless agony. So, Jet had to endure the pain until her Transcendent body naturally repaired itself in a few days. Cursing quietly, she made her way forward.

The emergency systems of the complex had been shut down, so the elevators weren't working. The stairwells had become strategic avenues for control over the building — most of them were in the hands of the turncoats, who were besieging the few underground levels where the remaining loyalists had barricaded themselves.

Jet wanted to help, but knew that revealing herself would only bring death and destruction upon them. So, she simply passed through the closed doors of an elevator and drifted down its dark shaft as a wraith.
The deeper she went, the louder the sounds of the battle were.
Eventually, she exited the shaft on one of the deepest levels of the government complex.

The turncoats seemed to have retreated from here temporarily, leaving behind dozens of corpses. Each of the dead men had been killed by one precise shot that struck the weak points of their armor with eerie precision.
The floor was slick with blood.
Jet coalesced into a human and studied the corpses with a dispassionate expression. Then, sensing an intense gaze, she looked into the depths of the long corridor.
“Do you think your bullets can harm me?"
A woman of unassuming appearance revealed herself from the shadows, lowering a combat rifle. She was wearing a dark suit of enchanted combat armor, and her cold gaze was both piercing and calm.
The woman regarded Jet for a moment, then bowed slightly.
“Saint Jet. I am Awakened Kim. We'be been told to expect you."
Jet headed toward Awakened Kim with measured steps.
"I take it the evacuation is not going well?"
The woman shook her head.
“Everything is on schedule. You are the last one to arrive, though, so we must hurry. Follow me, please."

Together, they left the bloodied corridor behind and proceeded to the doors of a massive cargo elevator. The doors had been locked, but the seal was broken — as were the doors themselves. Something immensely powerful seemed to have torn them apart.
A pair of winches had been installed at the mouth of the vertical shaft, steel cables falling into the darkness.

Awakened Kim proficiently strapped herself to a descender, slung the rifle to her back, and began to rappel down without saying anything. Jet simply turned into a wraith and followed her by levitating in the air. The shaft was quite long. As they descended into the depths of the earth, Jet asked in an ethereal, echoing voice:
"Are members of the Shadow Clan not susceptible to the plague?"
Awakened Kim glanced at her briefly.

"We are, even if our resistance to it is quite high. After all, each of us knows our Sovereign personally — unlike the subjects of the Longing Domain, for most of whom Lady Nephis is merely an abstract symbol. Still, we did lose a few agents to the Hunger Domain, already. It's just that identifying them is far easier for us."
She stopped for a moment to catch her breath and nodded to her forearm, where a small sliver of a serpent tattoo was visible between the edge of her vambrace and the lip of her black glove.
"The Mark of Shadows rejects them. It's hard to miss."
Jet smiled.

A method like that did not guarantee absolute safety, but the Shadow Clan was indeed more immune to the plague than anyone else in the world. Better yet, their lands were separated from the rest of the Human Domain by the Hollow Mountains. There were currently only three ways to reach the Forgotten Shore.

One was to cross the Hollow Mountains — even if Asterion was capable of doing that, it would take him a considerable amount of time. The second was to be carried across the realm boundary by Dark Dancer Revel, who had been marked by the Lord of Shadows.
And the third way...
Just then, they reached the bottom of the immensely long elevator shaft.
In front of them was a vast underground chamber, so tall that its ceiling could barely be seen far above.
And in the centre of that chamber, a black rift towered like a wound in the fabric of the world.
The Dream Gate of the Lord of Shadows. One of the seven Gates he could open, and the only one he had left behind for his servants to use.
Detaching herself from the descender harness, Awakened Kim looked at her watch.
“We still have thirteen minutes left. Everyone, hurry! We depart in ten!"

There were other people in the chamber, clad in suits of armor similar to hers. They were currently busy loading supply crates and various equipment on primitive alloy wagons, ready to push them into the Gate. There was someone else in the chamber, as well — someone Jet had not expected to see.
Or rather... something else?

A dreadful black steed was standing beside the Dream Gate, watching the entrance to the shaft with his frightening crimson eyes. There was something different about him today, though. As Jet studied the Terror, she noticed a familiar iron locket hanging on a black leather cord around his neck.
She raised an eyebrow.
"What's that horse doing here?"
Awakened Kim looked at the black stallion respectfully.
“Lord Nightmare just showed up."
Jet hesitated for a few moments, then decided to move on and asked a different question instead:
“And what will happen in thirteen minutes?"
Kim looked at her watch again.
"Twelve minutes now.’
She lingered for a moment and then said in a reserved tone:
"We are retreating from the waking world, and no one should be able to follow us. So, in twelve minutes, the Dream Gate will shut down. Today is the deadline set for us by our boss."

Which meant that they would be completely cut from the rest of humanity after passing through the Gate — the only member of the Shadow Clan who could travel between the worlds freely was Revel herself.
Jet sighed.
“Good thing I arrived on time, then."
The minutes seemed to stretch endlessly until, finally, it was time to leave.
Just then, Jet heard an ominous sound coming from the shaft.
It was the sound of countless wings beating as a swarm of crows flew down.
"Move, now!"
The wagons were pushed into the Gate. The members of the Shadow Clan dove into its darkness, as well — Awakened Kim was the last one to pass the threshold.
Jet did not want to have to kill her old mentor, so she rushed forward.
“What are you waiting for? Need a special invitation?"

The dreadful steed of the Lord of Shadows spared her a chilling look, then glanced at the shaft and snorted. Then, he followed Jet into the dark expanse of the Dream Gate. No sooner did they emerge on the other side than the black rift of the Gate rippled... and disappeared without a trace.
Jet let out a long sigh and leaned on the side of the monstrous horse, catching her breath.
She could relax a little, since she had finally escaped the clutches of the Dreamspawn. Around her, the Dark City of the Forgotten Shore basked in impenetrable darkness.

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2830.txt
content: Not long before that, Effie had a nice breakfast with her family. Little Ling was boasting about his studies, while her husband was listening to him quietly and chuckling from time to time.
“Oh? Did you hear that, dear? Our son is interested in history now. Didn't you mention that you were fond of history too, when you were little?"
Ling stared at her thoughtfully.
“Huh? Mom was little once?"
Effie scoffed.
“Yeah, well. I couldn't really go anywhere when I was a kid, so I read everything I could get my hands on. I liked history too."
Her husband took her hand.
“You hear that, rascal? Not only was your mom little once, but she was also a bookworm..."

Soon enough, the breakfast was over, and its warm atmosphere dissipated. Effie saw her husband off to the gates of their estate and kissed him goodbye. He had business to attend to in the city today, while she was supposed to depart for the Castle after handing Little Ling off to his tutor.
Instead of hurrying back inside, however, Effie remained motionless for a while, looking at the street.
Then, she sighed and turned away. Entering their home, she called Ling and sat him down in front of her.
“Hey there, dumpling. Were you excited for your lessons today?"
He hesitated for a few seconds, then asked instead of answering:
"What's wrong, mom?"
Her smile faltered for a moment.
"Wrong? Nothing is wrong. It's just that I want you to do something for me."
Little Ling nodded.
“Of course! What is it?"
Effie pulled the Black Beast Locket, which was hanging around her neck, out.
"You see... Uncle Sunny is away right now. So, Auntie Aiko is all alone in a huge castle. I was thinking that maybe you'll go keep her company for a bit... what do you think about that?"
Little Ling's eyes ignited with enthusiasm.
“Really? Can I really go? Really?!"
Effie nodded with a smile, then patted his head.
“Sure. Oh, and that castle? It's a bit scary, but actually... the castle is alive. It can even walk around."
The boy's eyes widened.
"Wait. Uncle Sunny had such a cool castle this whole time, and he never even invited me once?"
Effie chuckled.
“Well, you can go take a look now."
Little Ling raised his fists into the air.
"Yes! I can't wait to tell dad!"
A subtle shadow ran across Effie's face.
“You can't tell dad yet. Once Uncle Sunny is back, I'll come pick you up. You can report your findings to us then — like an explorer. Okay?"
Little Ling suddenly seemed unsure.
"Uh... what, we are leaving right now? What about dad? What about my lessons?"

Instead of answering, Effie placed her hand on his shoulder. The locket hanging around her neck shimmered and collapsed into a rain of sparks, which then flowed like a torrent into Little Ling's chest.

"It's a bit dark where the castle is, so if you want to go somewhere sunny, just hop inside the Beast Farm. Okay? Actually, why don't you go there right now? Take a walk and then nap a little. By the time you come out, you'll already be with Auntie Aiko."
Little Ling seemed frozen in place.
"Mom! You gave me a Memory! It's your locket, mom!"
Effie patted him on the head.
“Take good care of it for me, alright?"
Some time later, Ling was safely hidden inside the Black Beast Locket. Picking it up from the floor, Effie sighed and then closed her eyes.
The realm boundary parted in front of her, and she appeared in the waking world.
